seo.title: Google ADK Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for Google ADK agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using Google ADK face heightened compliance risks due to multi-agent systems handling dynamic pricing, product recommendations, and customer data across Gemini-powered agents. Common failure modes like circular dependencies and session state corruption can lead to inaccurate product claims, pricing errors, or mishandled personal data, violating FTC guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, and PCI DSS. Vex provides essential guardrails through before_agent_callback integration to ensure consistent, compliant agent behavior and prevent costly regulatory violations.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims must be substantiated with evidence; agents cannot make unsubstantiated superiority claims or misleading comparisons.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates agent outputs against a knowledge base of verified product specs before delegation, blocking unsubstantiated claims.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data handling requires explicit consent tracking and data minimization; agents must not retain PII beyond session needs.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces session state hygiene and consent verification in callbacks, preventing data leakage across agent handoffs.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment processing agents must never log card details and must use tokenized flows for all transactions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans tool responses for PCI violations like raw card data exposure and enforces tokenization before agent processing.

complianceRequirements.description: Product descriptions and checkout flows must support screen readers with proper semantic markup and alt text.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits generated content for WCAG 2.1 compliance, ensuring accessible language and structure in agent responses.

industryRisks.name: Inaccurate Dynamic Pricing
industryRisks.description: Gemini model switching causes inconsistent pricing calculations across agents, leading to under/overcharging.
industryRisks.example: Cart agent quotes $49.99, checkout agent charges $59.99 due to model drift, triggering refund disputes.

industryRisks.name: Unsubstantiated Product Claims
industryRisks.description: Multi-agent delegation creates conflicting product benefit statements without evidence validation.
industryRisks.example: Recommendation agent claims 'best battery life' without data, violating FTC guidelines.

industryRisks.name: Session State Corruption
industryRisks.description: Long conversations corrupt consent status or cart state, exposing PII or incorrect refunds.
industryRisks.example: User consent forgotten mid-session, leading to GDPR violation during order confirmation.

industryRisks.name: Payment Data Exposure
industryRisks.description: Tool format mismatches leak card details between payment and confirmation agents.
industryRisks.example: Payment agent logs raw PAN in shared state, breaching PCI DSS v4.0.

implementation.title: Pricing Accuracy Guardrail
implementation.description: Validate dynamic pricing calculations against catalog data before checkout agent activation.

implementation.title: FTC Claims Validator
implementation.description: Block unsubstantiated product claims using verified spec database before recommendation delegation.

implementation.title: Consent State Monitor
implementation.description: Prevent CCPA/GDPR violations by validating consent persistence across agent handoffs.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Google ADK in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: FashionCo deployed Google ADK for their multi-agent shopping experience: a discovery agent (Gemini 1.5 Pro) recommends outfits, a styling agent (Gemini 1.0) suggests sizes/combos, pricing agent calculates bundles, and checkout agent (Gemini 1.5 Flash) processes orders. During Black Friday, circular delegation between styling and pricing agents caused infinite loops, session corruption dropped consent flags, and model switching led to inconsistent 40% discount applications. Customers received products at wrong prices, unsubstantiated 'best-seller' claims appeared without inventory backing, and one agent logged partial card data in debug mode, creating PCI exposure.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex as before_agent_callback, FashionCo blocked 98% of pricing discrepancies, enforced FTC-compliant claims via spec validation, and maintained consent state across 500K sessions. Circular dependencies were detected and rerouted, reducing cart abandonment 37%. No PCI incidents occurred during peak traffic, avoiding $2.3M in potential fines while maintaining conversion rates.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK today with `pip install google-adk vex-sdk` and add before_agent_callback to productionize compliant Google ADK agents.
